title: decimal(p,s) — exact base-10 numerics
manager: An exact decimal number type — the number type for money and measurement. Binary floats drift (0.1 + 0.2 is not 0.3), which is why financial code in float languages is its own genre of bug. FSL's decimal makes 19.99 plus tax exact by construction, and a bounded decimal(p,s) — p digits, s after the point — stays inside the provable tier, so machines that move money can still be model-checked rather than merely tested.
cs: Fixed-point over BigInt scaled by 10^s; banker's (half-even) rounding default with modes enumerable; promotion into decimal is explicit only, never silent from float; bounded form sits in the large-finite/SMT band; overflow errors like the sized ints, with opt-in saturating.
